title: Scalus 2026: Maintenance, Dijkstra Readiness, Interoperability & Application Runtime

rationale.summary: Vote: NEEDS_MORE_INFO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), critical information needed for a responsible directional vote is still missing. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Scalus is an established open-source Cardano development platform built by Lantr Engineering over three years of continuous delivery." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide: independent ecosystem-value evidence packet; Provide: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score -0.24; confidence 78.1%.
rationale.summary_raw: Doctrine-aware reasoning layer nudged the score by -0.020 (clamped to ±0.05): The proposal is aligned with public-benefit developer infrastructure and includes a narrower scope, milestone framing, and some independently checkable adoption indicators, but several high-materiality claims about direct usage, governance controls, and prior delivery are asserted rather than evidenced inside the document. Given the large treasury request and BEACN's caution around evidence quality and treasury stewardship, a small negative nudge is warranted rather than a positive spending lean.
top_fixes: Provide: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity
missing_evidence: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Funding for a 9-month continuation of Scalus covering maintenance, Dijkstra hard-fork readiness, interoperability across JVM and JS/TS ecosystems, and a scoped application runtime.

Deliverables: Ongoing maintenance: bug fixes, dependency upkeep, security patches, and releases, Dijkstra readiness including Plutus V4 support, ledger rules, transaction-builder updates, conformance testing, nested transactions, accounts, and guard scripts, Improved JS/TS interfaces and documentation for components embedded in MeshJS, Evolution SDK, and Lucid Evolution, Java/Kotlin interoperability including Spring Boot and Ktor examples and cleaner integration with Cardano Client Lib and Yaci, Foundational runtime components including reactive workers, chain follower, and task scheduler, Reference applications, integration tests, early-user feedback, public reporting, third-party assurance, and milestone-based delivery
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Scalus is an established open-source Cardano development platform built by Lantr Engineering over three years of continuous delivery.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Scalus is used directly by Gummiworm L2, Bifrost, SugarRush, Vela, and DID/DIDComm, and its components are reused in MeshJS, Evolution SDK, Lucid Evolution, Cardano Client Lib, and YaciDevKit.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests ₳2,464,844 for 9 months, equivalent to about $394,375 at a $0.16/ADA reference rate, with no contingency.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal is a reduced resubmission of a prior Scalus proposal, cutting the ask from ₳8.503 million to about ₳2.465 million, reducing duration from 12 to 9 months, reducing FTE from 8.25 to 2.25, and removing the standalone L1 node, full L2 integration, and broad formal verification from scope.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Delivery is proposed to be milestone-based through audited SundaeSwap treasury contracts with an independent oversight board and third-party assurance.

Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The proposal says Scalus has 4,642 commits across 32 releases, 12 contributors, and Scalus.js reaches more than 20,000 monthly downloads.
missing: Independent evidence for: Scalus is used directly by Gummiworm L2, Bifrost, SugarRush, Vela, and DID/DIDComm, and its components are reused in MeshJS, Evolution SDK, Lucid Evolution, Cardano Client Lib, and YaciDevKit.
Independent evidence for: Delivery is proposed to be milestone-based through audited SundaeSwap treasury contracts with an independent oversight board and third-party assurance.

Independent evidence for: Dijkstra readiness is presented as necessary because the hard fork introduces Plutus V4 support, nested transactions, guard script types, and accounts affecting smart contract development, evaluation, transaction building, ledger rules, testing, and workflows.

Independent evidence for: Lantr Engineering says it has delivered every prior Scalus milestone on time and commits to public reporting, open community sessions, and on-time milestone delivery for treasury-funded work.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Scalus is an established open-source Cardano development platform built by Lantr Engineering over three years of continuous delivery." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Scalus is used directly by Gummiworm L2, Bifrost, SugarRush, Vela, and DID/DIDComm, and its components are reused in MeshJS, Evolution SDK, Lucid Evolution, Cardano Client Lib, and YaciDevKit." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
